page: Amazon Web Services g5g.xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz, with 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. For graphics-intensive workloads, it bundles a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. It does not include local NVMe storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard compute tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance landing in the average tier. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is also average, measuring 72.22 GB/sec for read operations and 34.75 GB/sec for write operations. However, the system exhibits weaker performance in memory-bound and single-threaded tasks, as reflected in its poor PassMark memory score and weak Geekbench single-core and multi-core results. Cryptographic performance is also limited, with a poor PassMark CPU encryption test result.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5g.xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile by combining energy-efficient ARM architecture with a dedicated NVIDIA GPU. This resource density makes it a viable option for entry-level graphics-accelerated applications, Android game streaming, and lightweight machine learning inference, though it is constrained by its 1 Gbps network baseline and lack of local storage. It is best suited for ARM-native graphics rendering, mobile application testing, and small-scale GPU-accelerated workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.xlarge is an ARM64-based, graphics-intensive server instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8.0 GB of system memory and integrates a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance in multi-core CPU tasks and large-block memory bandwidth, though it exhibits weaker single-threaded and cryptographic performance. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native graphics workloads and lightweight GPU acceleration. It is best suited for Android game streaming, mobile application testing, and entry-level machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM, optimized for entry-level graphics and mobile game streaming.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM for graphics workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 server combining energy-efficient Graviton processors with dedicated NVIDIA Turing graphics acceleration for mobile and graphics-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated cores
NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM for graphics acceleration
8.0 GB system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Nitro hypervisor architecture with no local storage included
Suited for Android game streaming, mobile app testing, and light GPU inference
